title: Easy Sesame Noodles
description: Silky Asian-style noodles coated in a creamy peanut-sesame sauce with hints of ginger and garlic. This restaurant-quality dish comes together in just 20 minutes and is perfect served warm or chilled.
ingredients: 8 oz gluten-free spaghetti or rice noodles
1/4 cup + 1 tablespoon creamy peanut butter (ingredient list should be just peanuts and salt)
1 tablespoon plus 1 teaspoon minced fresh ginger
1/4 teaspoon chile-garlic sauce (up to 1 teaspoon for extra heat)
instructions: Bring a large pot of water to a boil. Cook 8 oz gluten-free noodles according to package directions until al dente. Drain and rinse briefly under cool water to stop cooking and prevent sticking.

Whisk together 1/4 cup toasted sesame oil, 1/4 cup tamari, 1/4 cup + 1 tablespoon peanut butter, 2 tablespoons lemon juice, 2 tablespoons brown sugar, 1 tablespoon + 1 teaspoon minced ginger, 2 cloves minced garlic, and 1/4 teaspoon chile-garlic sauce in a medium bowl until smooth and well combined.
Add warm water 1 tablespoon at a time if the sauce is too thick, until it reaches a pourable consistency.
Add the cooked noodles to the sauce and toss thoroughly until every strand is evenly coated.
Divide among bowls and top with 1/4–1/3 cup chopped roasted peanuts, 1 tablespoon sesame seeds, and sliced scallions. Serve at room temperature or chilled.
notes: For gluten-free: use gluten-free spaghetti, rice noodles, or gluten-free soba noodles and ensure tamari is gluten-free certified.
For peanut allergy: substitute sunflower seed butter and sunflower seeds for a nut-free version.
Make ahead: These noodles are excellent cold. The sauce thickens in the fridge — loosen with a splash of tamari and a drizzle of sesame oil before serving.
Natural peanut butter may separate — stir it well before measuring to get the proper ratio of oil to solids.
